description: Dual full H-bridge motor driver — 46V, 2A per channel, logic-level inputs, enable pins, current sensing

pins.name: SENSE_A
pins.description: Current sense resistor connection for channel A. Connect 0.5Ω resistor to GND for current limiting, or short to GND to disable.
pins.requirements: Must not be left floating. Short to GND if current sensing not used.

pins.name: OUT1
pins.description: Motor driver output A1. Connect to one motor terminal.
pins.requirements: Add 100nF fast-recovery diodes (or Schottky) from each output to VS and GND for flyback protection.

pins.name: OUT2
pins.description: Motor driver output A2. Connect to other motor terminal of motor A.
pins.requirements: Add flyback diodes. OUT1/OUT2 drive motor A, OUT3/OUT4 drive motor B.

pins.name: VS
pins.description: Motor supply voltage. 4.5V to 46V. Powers the H-bridge outputs.
pins.requirements: 100nF bypass cap to GND. Separate from logic supply. Use thick PCB traces — carries full motor current.

pins.name: IN1
pins.description: Logic input for channel A direction control (H-bridge A). TTL-compatible.
pins.requirements: Do not leave floating. Drive from MCU GPIO. Both IN1 and IN2 HIGH = brake (both outputs LOW).

pins.name: ENA
pins.description: Enable for channel A. HIGH = enabled. LOW = both outputs disabled (high-Z). Can be used for PWM speed control.
pins.requirements: Pull HIGH (via jumper or GPIO) to enable. If used for PWM, connect to MCU PWM pin.

pins.name: IN2
pins.description: Logic input for channel A direction control.
pins.requirements: Do not leave floating. Control table: IN1=H/IN2=L = forward; IN1=L/IN2=H = reverse; IN1=IN2 = brake.

pins.name: GND
pins.description: Power ground. Common ground for VS, Vss, and motor return current.
pins.requirements: Multiple GND pins — connect all to common ground plane. High current flows through these pins.

pins.name: VSS
pins.description: Logic supply voltage. 4.5V to 7V. Powers the input logic and control circuits.

pins.name: IN3
pins.description: Logic input for channel B direction control.

pins.name: ENB
pins.description: Enable for channel B. HIGH = enabled. Can be used for PWM speed control of motor B.
pins.requirements: Pull HIGH or connect to PWM.

pins.name: IN4
pins.description: Logic input for channel B direction control.

pins.name: SENSE_B
pins.description: Current sense resistor connection for channel B. Short to GND if not used.
pins.requirements: Must not be left floating. Short to GND if current sensing not used.

required_passives.reason: Motor supply bypass — motor switching generates large current transients

required_passives.placement: From each output (OUT1–OUT4) to VS and GND
required_passives.reason: Flyback protection for inductive motor loads — REQUIRED or L298N will be damaged by inductive spikes

application_circuits.name: Dual DC motor control
application_circuits.description: VS: motor supply. VSS: 5V logic. IN1/IN2 + ENA for motor A. IN3/IN4 + ENB for motor B. 8 flyback diodes across outputs.

common_mistakes: Omitting flyback diodes causes L298N damage from motor back-EMF spikes — 8 diodes required
SENSE_A and SENSE_B must not be left floating — short to GND if not used
ENA/ENB must be HIGH for outputs to work — if both are floating/low, no motor movement
L298N has ~2V dropout across H-bridge — motor receives VS-2V, not VS
VS and VSS must share common GND — floating grounds cause erratic behavior
Maximum 2A continuous — exceeding this causes thermal shutdown or damage
Logic inputs must match VSS level — using 3.3V MCU with 5V VSS may not drive inputs HIGH reliably
notes: L298N has significant voltage drop (~2V) across H-bridge transistors. Actual motor voltage = VS − 2V. At high currents, the package gets very hot — heatsink required above 1A. For lower-voltage, more efficient alternatives consider DRV8833 (2.7–10.8V, lower dropout) or TB6612FNG.
